records.ety: [F. rfuge, L. refugium, fr. refugere to flee back; pref. re- + figere. SEe Fugitive.]

defs: Shelter or protection from danger or distress.
quotes: Rocks, dens, and caves! But I in none of these Find place or refuge.
We might have a strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us.

defs: That which shelters or protects from danger, or from distress or calamity; a stronghold which protects by its strength, or a sanctuary which secures safety by its sacredness; a place inaccessible to an enemy.
quotes: The high hills are a refuger the wild goats.
The Lord also will be a refuge for the oppressed.

defs: An expedient to secure protection or defense; a device or contrivance.
quotes: Their latest refuge Was to send him.
Light must be supplied, among gracefulrefuges, by terracing <?/<?/<?/ story in danger of darkness.
